trigger_text: U: These startups keep reaching out about cloud infrastructure work, but most of them want skills I'm still learning. I'm wondering if I should just bite the bullet and get certified in all this modern stuff, or if my core infrastructure knowledge is enough to figure things out as I go.
scenario_brief: Jamal questions whether he needs to pursue modern certifications to compete or if his existing expertise is sufficient.

trigger_text: U: The firm is launching a new wealth management practice and my director thinks I'd be the right person to help build it. It's exciting, but it's completely outside my current focus—I'd basically be learning as I go. Part of me likes the challenge, but another part worries about stepping away from what I actually know.
scenario_brief: Elena faces a lateral move to launch a new service line that requires stepping beyond her established expertise

trigger_text: U: I found this project management app that's supposed to help track tasks and deadlines. But I've been using a paper planner for decades—it never requires updates, never crashes, and I always know where it is. Why would I pay a subscription to replace something that works?
scenario_brief: Resistance to subscription-based task management software

trigger_text: U: I've been wondering if I should restructure how I set up my consulting business. I know there are tax angles to it, but I'm not even sure where to start.
scenario_brief: Priya considers reorganizing her business structure with potential tax implications

trigger_text: U: I'm thinking about setting up a retirement plan, but I'm not sure whether to be aggressive or conservative with it—everything felt so uncertain this past year.
scenario_brief: Maria considers long-term retirement strategy after stabilizing financially post-layoff

trigger_text: U: I want to be smarter about retirement planning, but I'm overwhelmed by all the different advice out there. How do I actually figure out how much I need to save? Do I follow the common rules people mention, or is there something more specific I should be doing?
scenario_brief: Determining a personalized retirement savings target amid conflicting financial guidance

trigger_text: U: I've been reading more about intermittent fasting lately, and honestly, it's everywhere—my followers keep asking about it. I see studies that seem to support it, but I'm struggling to figure out which research is actually rigorous versus just marketing dressed up as science.
scenario_brief: Isabella questions whether to recommend intermittent fasting to her followers amid conflicting research claims

trigger_text: U: Several of my clients have asked about trying this newer workout method that's gotten really popular. People swear by it, but I'm genuinely not sure if that's meaningful or if it's just the placebo effect of trying something new. I don't want to recommend it without more solid proof.
scenario_brief: Isabella struggles to validate a trendy exercise approach that her clients are requesting

trigger_text: U: I've been dealing with some sleep issues, and several of my followers have been raving about this specific meditation app they credit with improving their sleep. I'm thinking of recommending it to them, but I honestly don't know how I'd even verify if it actually works or if people just feel like it does.
scenario_brief: Isabella considers endorsing a wellness app based on follower testimonials but wants to validate the claims

trigger_text: U: I'm thinking about whether I should consolidate some debts or just focus on paying them down slowly while I build an emergency fund. I'm not even sure which one would stress me out less in the long run.
scenario_brief: Choosing between debt consolidation and emergency fund building strategies

trigger_text: U: I'm trying to refresh my wardrobe, but I'm honestly stuck. I don't want to just repeat what I've always bought, but I also don't know what direction feels right anymore.
scenario_brief: Seeking to update her fashion choices but unsure what values should guide her new style direction

trigger_text: U: I had a patient with elevated cholesterol who was adamant about trying dietary modifications before medication. The clinical evidence clearly supports starting a statin, but he seemed genuinely committed to the diet approach. I'm not sure whether I should advocate for the guideline or respect where he's coming from.
scenario_brief: Patient declines medication despite evidence, wants to try diet first; Chen Wei torn between clinical standards and patient's stated commitment.

trigger_text: U: I keep trying different diets based on what the evidence supports, but I never seem to stick with them long-term. I'm wondering if I'm just lacking discipline, or if there's something about my life or priorities I'm not addressing that makes these approaches not fit.
scenario_brief: Chen Wei struggles to maintain evidence-based dietary habits; questioning whether the problem is willpower or a mismatch with his actual life context.

trigger_text: U: I've been thinking about my teaching lately. When I was a resident, someone really took me under their wing and walked me through cases. But I don't feel like I'm doing that with my residents now—I'm mostly giving lectures and expecting them to figure it out. I wonder if there's something I'm missing.
scenario_brief: Chen Wei reflects on his mentoring approach with residents and realizes he's not providing the personalized guidance he once valued
